name: Acme Foundry, Inc.
what_they_make: Gray iron, ductile iron, and aluminum castings for automotive, industrial, and agricultural applications
who_they_sell_to: OEMs in automotive and heavy equipment industries, tier-1 and tier-2 suppliers, industrial manufacturers
key_differentiators: Tight tolerances on complex geometries
In-house pattern making and tooling
PPAP and APQP capabilities for automotive compliance
Domestic US manufacturer with fast lead times vs. overseas
common_customer_pain_points: Overseas supplier quality issues and long lead times
Need for engineering support during design phase
Regulatory and compliance traceability requirements
tone: Professional, straightforward, technically credible. Client is direct and no-nonsense.
past_topics: How to specify a casting order
notes: New POC is Savannah. Solid relationship. Responds well to technical content.

name: Benchmark Foam, Inc.
what_they_make: Custom-cut expanded polystyrene (EPS) foam products for packaging, construction insulation, and cold chain applications
who_they_sell_to: Product manufacturers needing custom protective packaging, contractors and builders, food and pharmaceutical cold chain companies
key_differentiators: Precision CNC foam cutting for complex shapes
Fast turnaround on custom orders
Sustainable EPS recycling program
Design engineering support for packaging optimization
common_customer_pain_points: Product damage during shipping
Expensive custom packaging from large suppliers
Long lead times from overseas foam suppliers
tone: Friendly and approachable, but technically credible. Jackie is the contact - has a good relationship with the team.
past_topics: EPS foam vs. alternatives for protective packaging
notes: Great relationship. Parted with sales team but kept marketing. Easy to work with.

name: Maxwell Lighting
what_they_make: Custom and specification-grade commercial lighting solutions for new construction and lighting retrofit projects
who_they_sell_to: Electrical contractors, architects and designers, building owners and facility managers, general contractors
key_differentiators: Specification-grade product knowledge and consulting
Strong local relationships with contractors and designers
Energy audit and ROI modeling for retrofit projects
Fast project turnaround and reliable delivery
common_customer_pain_points: Energy costs from outdated lighting
Compliance with energy codes (ASHRAE, Title 24)
Complexity of specifying the right fixtures for a project
tone: Consultative and educational. Owner is now a believer in SEO and content after seeing results. Very happy client.
avoid: Overly product-focused content without educational angle
past_topics: How to read a lighting spec sheet
Choosing color temperature for commercial spaces
notes: One of oldest/first clients. Built their site. Becky wants to add case studies. Very happy with marketing results.

name: Mectron Inspection Engineering
what_they_make: Custom automated inspection and vision systems for high-precision manufacturing environments, including gauging, vision, and part sorting systems
who_they_sell_to: Tier 1 and Tier 2 automotive suppliers, aerospace manufacturers, medical device manufacturers, precision machining shops
key_differentiators: Fully custom engineered inspection systems (not off-the-shelf)
Deep automotive and aerospace domain expertise
Integration with existing production lines
Long-term service and calibration support
common_customer_pain_points: Scrap and rework costs from missed defects
Customer audit failures due to inspection gaps
Labor costs and inconsistency of manual inspection
tone: Technical and credible. Long sales cycle (systems sell for ~$1.5M). Content should build credibility and trust over time, not push for quick conversion.
past_topics: Vision system vs. manual inspection ROI
How to spec a custom gauging system
notes: Great 2-year relationship. Slow to approve content. Machines sell for $1.5M - long sales cycle. Tariffs hitting hard right now - could be a content angle.

name: LobePro Rotary Pumps
what_they_make: Rotary lobe pumps for sanitary, industrial, and municipal applications, including food-grade and chemical-resistant configurations
who_they_sell_to: Food and beverage processors, municipal water and wastewater utilities, chemical processors, pharmaceutical manufacturers
key_differentiators: 3A Sanitary certified pumps for food-grade applications
Wide range of materials for chemical compatibility
Direct manufacturer support and technical sizing expertise
Parts availability and fast lead times vs. European competitors
common_customer_pain_points: Pump selection complexity for viscous or shear-sensitive products
Downtime from pump failures in critical processes
Sanitary compliance requirements in food/pharma
tone: Technical but accessible. Good content, but slow on email approvals. Content should lean into application-specific use cases.
avoid: Generic pump content that doesn't differentiate rotary lobe from centrifugal
past_topics: Sanitary pump CIP cleaning best practices
notes: Great content historically. Slow on email approval due to formatting. Parts sales dropped off - could be a pain point to address in content.

name: Winona Powder Coating, Inc.
what_they_make: Industrial powder coating services for metal components, including standard colors, custom colors, and specialty coatings
who_they_sell_to: Metal fabricators, OEMs, job shops, and manufacturers needing finished surface treatment on steel, aluminum, and other metals
key_differentiators: Fast turnaround (often 2-3 day standard, rush available)
Large batch capacity for production runs
Color matching and custom color development
Thorough surface prep included (sandblast, iron phosphate)
common_customer_pain_points: Slow turnaround from current coating suppliers holding up production
Inconsistent finish quality
Hidden costs from inadequate surface prep
tone: Straightforward and practical. Client is great about providing photos and feedback. Content can highlight real shop work.
past_topics: How to prep parts for powder coating
Choosing the right powder coat for outdoor applications
notes: Really good about providing photos and feedback. Great client for showing real work.

name: MW Piping Fabrication, LLC
what_they_make: Custom-fabricated piping systems and spools for industrial, oil & gas, and mechanical contractors, including carbon steel, stainless, and alloy pipe
who_they_sell_to: Mechanical contractors, industrial plant owners, refineries, chemical plants, and power generation facilities
key_differentiators: AWS-certified welders on staff
ASME B31.3 and B31.1 code compliance
In-house NDE/NDT testing
Fast turnaround on custom spool fabrication
common_customer_pain_points: Field welds causing schedule delays on construction projects
Finding a fabricator that can meet ASME code requirements
Quality documentation for owner/operator requirements
tone: Technical and code-focused. Recently very happy with marketing - just got a job because of organic search. Build on that momentum.
avoid: Content that implies they do work outside their core capabilities
past_topics: How to spec a piping spool order
notes: Built site live in 2025. Very happy. Just got a job from organic search. Was reluctant about content early on - now fully on board.
